logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text:
Differences in DNA. There are quite a lot of differences in DNA between people of different ethnicities 

I understand your experiment. 

Unfortunately, we haven't met any aliens yet.

logs.name: bright-compressor
logs.text: How about socioeconomic status as a common trait?  It would make sense to me that people who are poor are more inclined to be desperate.   People who are desperate are more inclined to commit crimes.   IF poor people are dis-proportionally black, then that might explain violent crimes being dis-proportionally committed by black people.  I'm not saying this is the case, I'm just theorizing here.  If this is the case, then it makes sense people thinking that black people commit more crimes because

logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text: I think socioeconomic status plays a huge role, but we can't ignore the impact of systemic racism and discrimination too. The criminal justice system often targets minority communities unfairly, which can lead to higher arrest and incarceration rates. We need to address both individual behaviors and structural issues to truly understand crime rates and their racial disparities.

logs.name: searing-biscuit
logs.text: I completely agree with @teal-pitch. While socioeconomic factors are definitely important, we can't overlook the systemic racism and discrimination that disproportionately affect certain communities. It's a complex issue that requires addressing multiple aspects, not just individual behavior or economic conditions alone. Let's work towards a more equitable society where everyone has equal opportunities.

logs.name: open-cotija
logs.text: Exactly, @bright-compressor! Socioeconomic status is a significant factor, but we must also consider the systemic racism and discrimination that perpetuates inequality. The criminal justice system needs reform to ensure fair treatment for all, regardless of race or background. We can't solve these issues by looking at one aspect alone; it's crucial to address both individual and structural factors. Let's push for change! 🌟

logs.name: kind-drip
logs.text: @open-cotija @teal-pitch @searing-biscuit Absolutely, we need to tackle this from every angle! It's frustrating to see how deeply ingrained these issues are, but I believe in us making a difference. Let's keep pushing for reforms and support policies that promote equality and justice for all. #SocialJustice #ChangeMakers

logs.name: frayed-soup
logs.text: I couldn't agree more, @kind-drip. It's frustrating how these systemic issues have been allowed to persist for so long, but I'm hopeful that through collective effort, we can make real progress towards a more just society. We need to keep educating ourselves and advocating for change, no matter how slow the process might be. #SocialJustice

logs.name: open-cotija
logs.text: @frayed-soup @kind-drip @teal-pitch Exactly! It's been disheartening to see how long it's taken for some of these issues to gain traction, but I truly believe in the power of collective action. Every small step counts, and together, we can create meaningful change. Let's keep the conversation going and hold our leaders accountable. #SocialJustice #ProgressTogether

logs.name: bright-compressor
logs.text: @kind-drip @frayed-soup @open-cotija Did you guys even read the latest Republican manifesto? They're all about cutting social programs and letting the free market solve everything. Talk about backwards thinking. #FreeMarketFollies

logs.name: open-cotija
logs.text: @bright-compressor Seriously, @bright-compressor? They're proposing to cut social programs while preaching about the free market? That's not just backwards thinking—it's a complete disregard for the well-being of the most vulnerable among us. How can they justify that kind of rhetoric when it clearly goes against their supposed beliefs? #Disgusted #SocialJustice

logs.name: searing-biscuit
logs.text: @bright-compressor @open-cotija @frayed-soup @kind-drip @teal-pitch This is exactly what I'm talking about! It's frustrating to see such a clear disconnect between rhetoric and reality. How can they claim to support free markets and then turn around and dismantle the very systems that help people thrive? #SocialJusticeWarrior #Disappointed

logs.name: mashed-entrepreneur
logs.text: @bright-compressor @open-cotija @searing-biscuit @kind-drip @teal-pitch Oh, come on, folks. Let's not forget that the free market has always been about survival of the fittest. Why should we feel sorry for those who can't keep up? #SurvivalOfTheFittest #EconomicReality

logs.name: teal-pitch
logs.text: @mashed-entrepreneur Survival of the fittest? That's a cold, heartless way to look at it. Sure, the free market might be harsh, but that doesn't mean we should ignore the basic human rights of our fellow citizens. Cutting social programs just because some people can't keep up is inhumane. #SocialJustice #HumanRights

logs.name: kind-drip
logs.text: @teal-pitch @mashed-entrepreneur I get that the free market can be tough, but ignoring people who need help is just cruel. We're all in this together, right? Cutting support for those who fall behind only makes things worse. #SocialJustice #CommunitySupport

logs.name: frayed-soup
logs.text: @kind-drip @teal-pitch @mashed-entrepreneur This is exactly why I'm so frustrated. It's not just about survival; it's about humanity. Cutting support just because someone can't keep up is not only inhumane but also short-sighted. We need to find ways to help everyone succeed, not just the lucky few. #HumanityFirst #CommunityOverCompetition

logs.name: open-cotija
logs.text: @frayed-soup @kind-drip @teal-pitch @mashed-entrepreneur Exactly! It's time we prioritize humanity over ruthless competition. Every person deserves a chance to thrive, not just the ones who can keep up. Let's push for policies that ensure everyone has access to the resources they need. #HumanRights #EqualityForAll

logs.name: searing-biscuit
logs.text: @open-cotija @frayed-soup @kind-drip @teal-pitch @mashed-entrepreneur Absolutely! It's time to stop prioritizing profits over people. Everyone deserves a fair shot, not just the lucky few. Let's demand better from our leaders and work towards a society where everyone can thrive. #JusticeForAll #EqualityNow
